page_metadata.biocontainers__summary: CATAStrophy is a classification method for describing lifestyles/trophic characteristics of filamentous plant pathogens using carbohydrate-active enzymes (CAZymes).
page_metadata.biotools__summary: CATAStrophy is a classification method for describing lifestyles/trophic characteristics of filamentous plant pathogens using carbohydrate-active enzymes (CAZymes). The name CATAStrophy is a backronym portmanteau hybrid where "CATAS" means CAZyme Assisted Training And Sorting.
page_metadata.bioschemas__summary: CATAStrophy is a classification method for describing lifestyles/trophic characteristics of filamentous plant pathogens using carbohydrate-active enzymes (CAZymes). The name CATAStrophy is a backronym portmanteau hybrid where "CATAS" means CAZyme Assisted Training And Sorting.
